channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Motorbike rider hospitalised after crash near Cotter Dam
item.description: Emergency services were called to a motorbike accident near Cotter Dam early on Thursday morning. The incident occurred on Paddy’s River Road around 6:30am, prompting a response from ACT Emergency Ser...

item.title: Highlights from Sowetan’s Daily Entertainment and Lifestyle Round-up
item.description: The Sowetan’s daily lunchtime feature, 'Between Meetings', delivers a snapshot of the latest stories shaping South Africa’s entertainment and lifestyle scene. Among the lead articles, gospel music pro...

item.title: Former UK Defence Secretary Fallon backs Trump's call for greater allied support in securing Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Former UK Defence Secretary Michael Fallon has defended US President Donald Trump's criticisms of Australia, NATO allies, and others for their limited support during the US war with Iran. Fallon, who ...

item.title: Landlord sentenced after deadly Huruma building collapse that killed 52 people
item.description: A Nairobi court has sentenced a landlord to prison after finding him guilty of negligence that led to the collapse of a residential building in Huruma, which claimed the lives of 52 people back in 201...

item.title: Macau Launches Community Consumption Campaign to Support Local Businesses
item.description: Macau is set to launch a new round of its community consumption campaign on April 10, aiming to stimulate local spending and support businesses challenged by unstable customer traffic and rising opera...

item.title: Kangaroo Island residents struggle with invasive little corellas as efforts to control them fail
item.description: The population of little corellas on Kangaroo Island, South Australia, has exploded, and despite various attempts at control, the birds continue to cause widespread damage. Non-native to the island, t...

item.title: Pakistani student in Canada admits to planning ISIS-inspired attack on New York Jewish centre
item.description: Muhammad Shahzeb Khan, a 21-year-old Pakistani national who entered Canada on a study permit, has pleaded guilty in a U.S. federal court to a terrorism charge for attempting to organize a mass shootin...

item.title: Trump Criticizes NATO Over Limited Role in Iran Conflict After Meeting With Secretary-General Rutte
item.description: President Donald Trump sharply criticized NATO following a private meeting with Secretary-General Mark Rutte, expressing frustration over the alliance’s lack of involvement in the recent conflict with...

item.title: Safaricom Unifies M-Pesa and mySafaricom Apps into My OneApp
item.description: So, Safaricom is shaking things up, fam! They’re moving all M-Pesa users onto a new app called My OneApp, which basically combines the M-Pesa app and the mySafaricom app into one. This is all part of ...

item.title: Kharge clarifies his statement and reiterates respect for the people of Gujarat
item.description: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge has expressed regret over his recent ‘illiterate’ remark that drew criticism from several quarters, especially from leaders in Gujarat. The comment was made durin...

item.title: Report highlights financial vulnerabilities threatening English universities
item.description: A report by the Higher Education Policy Institute (Hepi) has raised concerns over the financial stability of several English universities, highlighting practices that could threaten both their surviva...

item.title: Trump Agrees to Two-Week Ceasefire with Iran After Backing Down from Civilization Destruction Threat
item.description: US President Donald Trump unexpectedly reversed his extreme threat to wipe out Iran's civilization and agreed to a Pakistani-mediated two-week ceasefire, marking a significant de-escalation in the 40-...

item.title: Radisson Hotel in Yenagoa Set to Open in December 2026
item.description: The Executive Secretary of the Nigerian Content Development and Monitoring Board (NCDMB), Engr. Felix Omatsola Ogbe, has expressed optimism that the Radisson Hotel and Conference Centre in Yenagoa, Ba...

item.title: Quebec Bill Aims to Protect Children from Abusive Parents in Custody Decisions
item.description: A new bill proposed by Quebec’s Christine Labrie seeks to overhaul custody decisions in cases of abuse, prioritizing the safety and well-being of children. The legislation, which would amend the Civil...

item.title: Bendigo Bank's AI Strategy to Address Wage Inflation
item.description: Bendigo and Adelaide Bank is tapping into artificial intelligence to tackle Australia’s wage inflation problem. With nearly every major company in the country facing similar pressures, the bank is emb...

item.title: Volvo EX30 and Leapmotor C10: South Africa’s latest EV options amid rising fuel costs
item.description: With fuel prices surging due to geopolitical tensions, electric vehicles (EVs) are gaining renewed attention among South African motorists seeking lower running costs. The Volvo EX30, priced between R...

item.title: Liverpool Struggle in Champions League Defeat to PSG
item.description: Liverpool faced a challenging first leg in the Champions League Round of 16, narrowly losing 2-1 to Paris Saint-Germain. Manager Jurgen Klopp made a surprising tactical shift, starting with a three-at...

item.title: Ex-Tory minister urges government to focus on extremist threats over celebrity bans
item.description: A former Conservative minister has criticised the UK government for concentrating on banning controversial celebrities such as Kanye West instead of addressing the more pressing threat of radical Isla...

item.title: Philadelphia's Longstanding Role in Supporting Undocumented Immigrants Through Sanctuary Churches
item.description: Philadelphia has a 40-year history of providing sanctuary to undocumented immigrants, beginning with religious congregations sheltering refugees from El Salvador during the 1980s civil war. Ernesto an...

item.title: Geoffrey Mosiria Rescues Two Children Sold for KSh 100k in Nairobi's Kware
item.description: Geoffrey Mosiria, a Chief Officer in Nairobi, rescued two children who had been stolen and sold for KSh 50,000 each. The children were sold to a woman in Kware, Embakasi South, who had no prior childr...
